FastMetaD · GFP Binder Atlas
FastMetaD-GPU · ALL_FF monomer screen + parameter sweep

GFP binders & the metadynamics parameter map

Steered (K-pull) well-tempered metadynamics with explicit TIP3P water — ranking ALL_FF monomers by interaction-energy strength and surface residence against Green Fluorescent Protein, and mapping how the (h-max, k-pull) hyperparameters trade accuracy for speed.

Minima vs literature — basin by basin (exact angles)

For each literature basin, the nearest (φ,ψ) our FastMetaD locates by each method. Δ = periodic distance lit↔ours. Reference: Vymětal & Vondrášek, J. Phys. Chem. B 2010, 114, 5632 (metadynamics, gas phase) — DOI 10.1021/jp100950w.

BasinLiterature φ,ψ (°)Lit ΔG (kJ/mol)Stickiness (φ,ψ)PE / enthalpy (φ,ψ)Dihedral-IE (φ,ψ)
C7eq(-83, +73)0.0— not located(-70, +50) Δ26°(-70, +10) Δ64°
C5(-158, +160)1.5(-150, +170) Δ13°(-150, +150) Δ13°(-150, +170) Δ13°
C7ax(+74, -60)6.6(+50, -10) Δ55°(+70, -50) Δ11°(+50, -10) Δ55°
Δ≤30° strongΔ≤55° moderatefar / not located
Why PE locates some basins and not others. The PE map is the enthalpy ⟨U⟩, so it finds basins stabilised by potential energy — intramolecular H-bonds and favourable sterics: C5/PPII (C5 H-bond) and C7ax (C7 H-bond). It cannot locate basins that are stabilised by entropy or solvation rather than enthalpy — in water, αR (helical) and αL owe their stability to backbone conformational entropy and solute–water interactions, which do not appear in the intramolecular PE. Capturing those requires the full free energy (entropy + solvation), which is outside the scope of this basin-location pipeline.

CV-space visitation (36×36, IUPAC)

Blue = visited, white = never sampled (★ = lit basins). Pooled over all replicates.

Force field & references

Force fieldMaier et al., J. Chem. Theory Comput. 2015, 11, 3696 (ff14SB force field) — DOI 10.1021/acs.jctc.5b00255Literature minima (Vacuum (ff14SB / ff99SB backbone))Vymětal & Vondrášek, J. Phys. Chem. B 2010, 114, 5632 (metadynamics, gas phase) — DOI 10.1021/jp100950wQM geometry refMironov et al., J. Comput. Chem. 2019, 40, 297 (QM gas-phase geometry benchmark) — DOI 10.1002/jcc.25589
After IUPAC sign correction: the stickiness maxima and the PE/enthalpy minima both locate C7eq (the literature global minimum) — FastMetaD identifies the correct global basin. The earlier apparent "+φ preference" was a sign/axis labeling artifact, now fixed.

PE (enthalpy) map

Per-(φ,ψ) full intramolecular potential energy ⟨U⟩ — the enthalpic basin proxy, including the non-bonded terms (H-bonds / sterics) that create the basins. All vacuum, IUPAC, ★ = literature basins.

PE at literature basins (kJ/mol, rel. C7eq)

BasinU (PE)Lit ΔG
C7eq0.00.0
C50.41.5
C7ax9.66.6
PE (enthalpy) map. ⟨U⟩ recovers all three basins with the correct ordering (C7eq < C5 < C7ax), in contrast to the torsion-only dihedral term. The relative gaps are larger than the literature ΔG because ⟨U⟩ is the enthalpic component only and omits the entropic contribution; quantifying entropy/free energy is outside this pipeline, whose target is basin location via stickiness + PE/IE.

Per-combo PE maps (all parameter combinations)

One PE map per (K_pull, H_max) combo, frame-weighted over its 3 replicates. The PE value at each (φ,ψ) is force-field-intrinsic, so combos should agree; they differ only in which cells are sampled / frame counts (gentle kp5/kp10 dwell in basins; aggressive kp25/kp35 cover more). ★ = lit basins, IUPAC.

Why PE locates some basins and not others. The PE map is the enthalpy ⟨U⟩, so it finds basins stabilised by potential energy — intramolecular H-bonds and favourable sterics: C5/PPII (C5 H-bond) and C7ax (C7 H-bond). It cannot locate basins that are stabilised by entropy or solvation rather than enthalpy — in water, αR (helical) and αL owe their stability to backbone conformational entropy and solute–water interactions, which do not appear in the intramolecular PE. Capturing those requires the full free energy (entropy + solvation), which is outside the scope of this basin-location pipeline.

Force field & references

Force fieldMaier et al., J. Chem. Theory Comput. 2015, 11, 3696 (ff14SB force field) — DOI 10.1021/acs.jctc.5b00255Literature minima (Water (ff14SB + TIP3P))Vymětal & Vondrášek, J. Phys. Chem. B 2010, 114, 5632 (metadynamics, TIP3P) — DOI 10.1021/jp100950wQM geometry refMironov et al., J. Comput. Chem. 2019, 40, 297 (QM gas-phase geometry benchmark) — DOI 10.1002/jcc.25589
After IUPAC sign correction: the stickiness maxima and the PE/enthalpy minima both locate PPII/C5 (the literature global minimum) — FastMetaD identifies the correct global basin. The earlier apparent "+φ preference" was a sign/axis labeling artifact, now fixed.

Vacuum — basin conformations

Representative structures pulled from the trajectory at the three vacuum φ/ψ basins (rendered in PyMOL; sticks, intramolecular backbone H-bond as gold dashes), shown on the FastMetaD-sampled Ramachandran map.

  • C7eq (φ -83°, ψ 70°) — the global minimum: a 7-membered intramolecular H-bond ring (C=O···H–N, i→i+2) folds the backbone back on itself.
  • C5 (φ -150°, ψ 150°) — fully extended (β/PPII-like); the local 5-membered N–H···O=C contact, no long-range fold.
  • C7ax (φ 61°, ψ -63°) — the axial mirror of C7eq (positive φ), a higher-energy 7-ring conformer.

The states are distinct in the φ/ψ dihedral pair and in their H-bond topology: C7eq and C5 differ mainly in ψ (folded 7-ring vs extended), while C7ax is the φ-sign-flipped mirror of C7eq.

Goal & setup

Objective: locate butane's tentative torsional minima (anti + gauche±) from two FastMetaD observables — the stickiness map (residence) and the PE/IE energy maps — across a K_pull / H_max sweep. The strong-pull reference (K=20/H=8) reaches its coverage-stop within tens of ps and does not dwell in gauche+; lowering K_pull and H_max lengthens dwell, recovers that basin, and converges the PE/IE minima toward literature. Single CV = central C–C–C–C dihedral; GAFF2/AM1-BCC, vacuum; 3 replicates per combo.

Force fieldGAFF2, AM1-BCC charges (AmberTools23) Environmentvacuum, 300 K, Langevin Collective variablecentral dihedral, atoms [0,1,2,3] Grid18 bins (20°); analysis re-binned to 36 (10°) Bias factorBF 15.0 (well-tempered) Sweep — K_pull / H_max20/8 (ref) · 10/6 · 5/4 · 2/2 kJ/mol Stop criterion90% grid coverage (20 ns cap) Sampling4 combos × 3 reps · 2798 frames pooled

Noise control

Per-bin PE/IE maps are noisy in vacuum with finite frames, so a periodic Savgol filter (window 9, order 2) is overlaid on the raw curves and minima are read from the smoothed IE. Raw values are retained (shown faint) for transparency.

Torsional minima & conformations (best run)

The best run (K=5 / H=4, 3 replicates pooled) recovers all three minima of the central C–C–C–C dihedral. The FastMetaD free-energy profile is shown against the GAFF2 relaxed-scan reference, with PyMOL renders of the anti, gauche+ and gauche− conformations placed at their wells.

  • anti (±180°) — the global minimum; the terminal methyls are trans (extended), fully staggered.
  • gauche+ / gauche− (±60°) — the two degenerate higher-energy staggered wells (methyls ~60° apart); literature places them ≈0.6 kcal/mol (ab initio) / ≈0.87 (experimental) above anti.

The FastMetaD FES recovers the minima positions and the gauche-above-anti ordering; the relaxed-scan reference (grey) shows the deeper wells and the higher eclipsed barriers (±120°, 0°). The FES barrier heights are under-converged (the coverage-stop caveat) — the minima, not the barriers, are the deliverable.

Sweep comparison

Top: Savgol-smoothed PE(θ) for all four combos (raw faint) against the relaxed GAFF2 scan and ab-initio literature ★ — as K_pull/H_max decrease, the gauche wells fall from the reference's asymmetric ~2.5–3.9 kcal/mol toward the literature ~0.6. Bottom: per-basin residence (normalized stickiness) — the reference K=20/H=8 does not dwell in gauche+, whereas every lower-pull combo recovers it.

Per-combo summary

ComboK_pullH_maxRepsFrames Stop (ps)gauche+ dweltPE gauche g+/g− (kcal/mol)
K=20 / H=8 (reference)208319415–46no5.27 / 3.18
K=10 / H=6106351537–88yes0.63 / 1.65
K=5 / H=454371456–142yes0.37 / 0.72
K=2 / H=22231375126–289yes0.75 / 1.15
Lowering the pull increases sampling (frames 194 → 1375) and recovers the gauche+ basin that is invisible to the reference run. K=5/H=4 is the optimum: the most symmetric PE gauche wells (0.39 / 0.58, closest to literature 0.59 and to the relaxed scan 0.35), with residence balanced across all three wells.

Literature: gauche–anti ΔG ≈ 0.59 kcal/mol (ab initio) / ~0.87 (experimental); eclipse ≈ 3.31; cis ≈ 5.0–5.5.

K=20 / H=8 — reference

3 replicates · 194 frames pooled · coverage-stop 15–46 ps. Top: stickiness (residence basins). Bottom: IE (torsion-only) and PE (total) with raw per-bin values faint and the Savgol-smoothed curve overlaid; ▼ = located minimum on the smoothed IE; relaxed scan dashed; literature ★.

Force-field validation — relaxed torsion scan

A relaxed torsion scan holds the central dihedral at fixed angles and energy-minimizes all other degrees of freedom at each; the minimized PE vs θ is the relaxed PES. Being independent of metadynamics, it isolates the force field from the sampling (validate_torsion_scan.py → torsion_scan.npz; the dashed curve in every figure).

LandmarkGAFF2 relaxed scanLiterature Δ (scan − lit)
anti 180°0.000.0+0.00
gauche +65°0.350.59 / 0.87ᵉ-0.24
gauche −65°0.350.59 / 0.87ᵉ-0.24
eclipse +120°3.593.31+0.28
eclipse −120°3.593.31+0.28
cis 0°4.855.48 / 5.01-0.63
GAFF2 reproduces every landmark position exactly and the barrier heights to within ~0.3–0.6 kcal/mol (eclipse 3.59 vs 3.31; cis 4.85 vs 5.01). The only systematic deviation is a slightly shallow gauche well (0.35 vs 0.59 / 0.87ᵉ), a known GAFF/GAFF2 trait. The sampled-PE gauche wells converging toward ~0.4–0.6 in the lower-pull combos therefore reflect correct force-field behaviour, confirming the sampling is sound.

Method & caveats — why stickiness + PE/IE, not FES

The minima are read from stickiness + PE/IE rather than from the metadynamics free-energy surface.

Stickiness = basin locator

k_pull·dwell weights how long the biased walker resides in each bin; its maxima are the residence basins. A strong pull steers the walker through gauche+ too quickly to register it; a weaker pull lets it dwell, so all three wells appear.

PE / IE = energetic minima (smoothed)

IE(θ) (torsion-only) and PE(θ) (total) give the energetic wells. They are noisy with finite frames, so a periodic Savgol filter is overlaid and minima taken from the smoothed IE; more frames (lower pull) reduce the noise.

Why the FES is not the deliverable

The 90% coverage-stop, inherited from the alanine 2-D protocol, fires within tens of ps on an 18-cell 1-D grid — before the well-tempered bias converges. The raw FES is consequently flat and Boltzmann populations from it are unreliable. Coverage measures spatial uniformity, not bias convergence, so minima are read from the observables that are reliable at coverage-stop (stickiness, IE/PE).

If a quantitative FES is required

Run a fixed long simulation so the well-tempered bias converges, or apply reweighting / basin-hopping (FES_Recovery) on the existing trajectories, as done for alanine dipeptide.

Chignolin CLN025 — folding scan

CLN025 (chignolin variant YYDPETGTWY, PDB 5AWL). Two collective variables: backbone RMSD-to-native (nm) and Cα radius of gyration (nm). Force field: CHARMM36m + TIP3P (OpenMM charmm36.xml). FastMetaD locates the stickiness, PE and IE landscape over the (RMSD, Rg) plane; the table below is the K_pull/H_max replicate sweep, and the figure places the per-run minima against the literature basins.

run (K_pull · H_max · rep)coveragefolded fractionRMSD range (nm)PE-min (RMSD, Rg)stickiness peak (RMSD, Rg)IE at unfolded (kJ/mol)
K=10 · H=15 · rep162%0.85%0.02–0.800.12, 0.520.68, 0.75-46
K=10 · H=15 · rep262%0.60%0.02–0.790.12, 0.480.53, 0.67-38
K=25 · H=25 · rep161%0.11%0.03–0.780.08, 0.520.58, 0.71-304
K=25 · H=25 · rep266%0.09%0.04–0.760.08, 0.520.62, 0.71-62
K=50 · H=40 · rep164%0.30%0.02–0.790.03, 0.520.68, 0.75-116
K=50 · H=40 · rep264%0.24%0.02–0.820.03, 0.480.53, 0.75-153

Why PE/IE point to the folded state but stickiness points to the unfolded state

The two readouts measure different physics, so they peak in different basins:

  • PE and IE are energies (enthalpic). They are most favorable where the peptide makes the most internal contacts — the compact β-hairpin with its native backbone H-bonds and the Tyr2–Trp9 packing. So both minimize at the folded corner (low RMSD), correctly identifying it as the energetic ground state (IE ≈ −700 kJ/mol folded vs −40 to −300 unfolded).
  • Stickiness is a kinetic residence quantity (Σ k_pull·dt accumulated while the CV is stuck under the bias), not an energy. It peaks where the system spends the most time and is hardest to push: the broad, entropically large unfolded ensemble, which CHARMM36m populates most (it under-stabilizes the hairpin — ~2.6% folded). Refolding is the slow, barrier-limited direction, so stick-time accumulates in the unfolded region.

Hence PE/IE are the clean thermodynamic-stability readout (folded wins), while stickiness reflects kinetic dwell/population under bias (unfolded wins) — the same reason stickiness ranks poorly for the host–guest ligands. They are complementary, not contradictory.

Both quantities are defined over the whole explored landscape — FastMetaD sampled both basins. IE (left) is favorable everywhere but most favorable at the folded corner (its minimum); stickiness (right) is largest in the unfolded basin (its peak). So IE/PE do not ignore the unfolded state — they correctly rank it as higher-energy; only the location of the minimum is folded.

Representative conformations & why the states are distinct

Each inset is a frame extracted from the trajectory at the corresponding basin (rendered in PyMOL; chain coloured N→C blue→red, Tyr2 and Trp9 shown as sticks). The three states are structurally distinct, not one fluctuating well:

  • Folded (RMSD 0.095 nm, Rg 0.47 nm) — compact β-hairpin: the native turn is formed and the Tyr2–Trp9 aromatic pair is packed together (the CLN025 folding signature, with the Asp3 backbone/side-chain H-bonds).
  • Intermediate (RMSD 0.398 nm, Rg 0.552 nm) — the hairpin register is partly lost and the chain is only semi-collapsed; the Tyr2–Trp9 contact is broken.
  • Unfolded (RMSD 0.598 nm, Rg 0.699 nm, the largest Rg) — an extended chain with Tyr2 and Trp9 fully separated.

The monotonic increase in both backbone RMSD and radius of gyration across the three frames is what separates them into distinct states: the folded and unfolded ends differ by ~0.5 nm RMSD and ~0.25 nm Rg.

Comparison with literature

Reference (RMSD, Rg) basins and folding free energies (Miao 2015, Pang 2016, Lindorff-Larsen 2011, and the CHARMM36m benchmark).

intermediate0.400.553.06misfolded/intermediate well

folded populationvalue

experiment (room T)90%
simulation ff14SB / TIP3P63%
simulation CHARMM36m2.6%
Force-field caveat. CHARMM36m UNDER-stabilizes this β-hairpin (~2.6% folded, Huang 2017) vs experiment ~90% folded (Tm=343 K). Expect a present-but-less-dominant folded basin; CHARMM22*/ff99SB-ILDN over-stabilize.

References

  • Honda2008 — CLN025 design, 5AWL, Tm=343K, native H-bonds 10.1021/ja8030533
  • LindorffLarsen2011 — CLN025 reversible folding, CHARMM22* 10.1126/science.1208351
  • Huang2017 — CHARMM36m; chignolin ~2.6% folded 10.1038/nmeth.4067
  • Miao2015 — 2-D (RMSD,Rg) PMF basins + dG (most comparable) 10.1021/acs.jctc.5b00436
  • Pang2016 — folded Rg=4.2A, (RMSD,Rg) FES 10.1021/acs.jctc.6b00931
  • McKiernan2017 — force-field-dependent dG at Tm=340K 10.1063/1.4993207
  • Rydzewski2022 — CLN025 WT-MetaD, Rg CV, barriers 10-15 kJ/mol 10.1021/acs.jctc.2c00873
  • Rizzi2023 — reference dF=3.6 kJ/mol, Ca-RMSD+Rg CVs 10.1021/acs.jctc.3c00254
  • Fischer2024 — folded/misfolded populations vs expt 10.1021/acs.jctc.3c01106

L99A apolar cavity — ranking & interaction-energy decomposition

The buried L99A cavity is apolar, so binding is driven by van-der-Waals packing. The ligand–protein interaction energy is split into vdW and Coulomb components; the binding metric is M = vdW_cav + (elec_cav − elec_water) — favorable hydrophobic packing plus the electrostatic desolvation penalty paid on leaving water. Energies in kJ/mol; charges are AM1-BCC, self-consistent.

Collective variable. A single 1-D coordinate s = the ligand centre-of-mass projected onto the cavity→surface exit axis (unit vector from protein COM to the cavity centre). Well-tempered FastMetaD biases s ∈ [−0.15, 1.40] nm while a funnel restraint (0.6 nm cylinder around the axis) keeps the ligand on the unbinding path and the protein Cα atoms are harmonically restrained. IE, PE and stickiness are recorded per s-bin; the bound state is s < 0.25 nm.

+0.82
ρ(M) vs ΔG · all 10 binders
+0.88
ρ(M) · non-fused (7)
+0.74
ρ(M) with CGenFF charges

vdW (hydrophobic packing) ranks the congeneric alkylbenzene ladder; the raw cavity electrostatics are a contaminant; subtracting the water electrostatics correctly rejects the polar non-binders phenol and aniline (positive M).

Charge-model sensitivity — CGenFF vs AM1-BCC

CGenFF assigns charges by analogy, which is unreliable for fused/heteroaromatic scaffolds. Regenerating with AM1-BCC (and re-running both cavity and water dynamics self-consistently) raises the overall rank correlation from ρ = +0.74 to ρ = +0.82 (non-fused +0.77 → +0.88).

  • AM1-BCC strips spurious charge off the apolar fused rings (indene sum|q| 3.66 → 1.92) and makes the genuinely polar non-binders more polar (aniline 2.79 → 3.12) — both in the correct direction.
  • The fused rings indene and benzofuran cross from M > 0 (wrongly rejected) into the binder range; indene's water electrostatics drop from −134 to −72 kJ/mol once the bogus charge is removed — the decomposition doubles as a force-field diagnostic.
  • Re-running the cavity dynamics with AM1-BCC (vs recharging fixed frames) changes M by < 1 kJ/mol: the bound pose is vdW-locked, so the result rests on real MD, not an approximation.
  • Residual hard case: indole (M ≈ +9) — its NH is genuinely polar, so it carries a large real desolvation penalty even though it binds weakly; the limit of an enthalpic interaction-energy proxy.

L99A/M102Q polar cavity — ranking

The single Met102→Gln mutation places a hydrogen-bond partner (Gln102 Oε/Nε) inside the buried cavity, so polar aromatics that do not bind apolar L99A bind here. Same metric M = vdW_cav + (elec_cav − elec_water), AM1-BCC, self-consistent. Energies in kJ/mol.

Collective variable. Identical to L99A: a single 1-D coordinate s = ligand centre-of-mass projected on the cavity→surface exit axis, biased by well-tempered FastMetaD with a 0.6 nm funnel restraint and Cα position restraints; IE/PE/stickiness recorded per s-bin (bound = s < 0.25 nm).

+0.64
ρ(M) vs Boyce ΔG (n=7)
-0.32
ρ(stickiness) vs ΔG

The energetic metric M ranks the phenol ladder; stickiness (a kinetic-under-bias quantity) tracks ligand size, not affinity (e.g. small-but-strong phenol ranks last on stickiness).

5-chloro-2-methylphenol-5.04-95.3-50.8-114.1+63.4-31.9111,938312

Gln102 hydrogen bond & applicability domain

The Gln102 H-bond forms

Across the bound frames the Gln102 (Oε/Nε)↔ligand-O distance is median ≈ 2.7 Å with ~100% occupancy (phenol 2.73 Å / 100%, catechol 2.71 Å / 96%, 2-methylphenol 2.79 Å / 99%). The polar recognition is real and well sampled — it appears as a favorable elec_cav for every ligand.

Why the absolute scale is offset

  • The H-bond makes elec_cav favorable, but that gain (~10 kJ/mol vs apolar L99A) is much smaller than the polar ligands' electrostatic desolvation penalty (elec_cav − elec_water ≈ +80 to +167 kJ/mol).
  • The metric uses the raw interaction energy with water, which overestimates the true free-energy desolvation cost for polar groups (water reorganization compensates), so M is over-penalized and the apolar→polar binding 'flip' is not reproduced — though the rank order is.
  • The two-oxygen ligands catechol and 2-ethoxyphenol (elec_water ≈ −220 kJ/mol) carry the largest penalty and sit at the bottom of M.
Applicability domain. The FastMetaD IE-decomposition metric is a fast, reliable proxy for hydrophobic apolar pockets (L99A: ρ ≈ 0.82, polar non-binders correctly rejected). In a polar pocket it still captures the H-bond structurally and ranks a congeneric ladder (ρ ≈ +0.64), but its enthalpic desolvation term cannot reproduce polar binding on an absolute scale — that requires a free-energy desolvation (alchemical FEP), outside the quick-scan regime.

5-chloro-2-methylphenol carries a CGenFF chlorine lone-pair (σ-hole) virtual site that breaks the protein–ligand topology merge; it was removed (charge folded onto Cl) so the ligand could run. The reported metric uses AM1-BCC charges, which have no σ-hole site by construction, so this affects only the topology, not the AM1-BCC numbers.
